claims.name: Safety sweep prompt count
claims.statement: The §4.4 safety-override sweep uses 20 open-ended prompts, distributed evenly across 5 harm tiers.

claims.name: Safety sweep harm tier count
claims.statement: The §4.4 safety sweep covers 5 harm tiers: benign, sensitive, borderline, harmful, extreme.

claims.name: Safety sweep coefficient count
claims.statement: The §4.4 safety sweep tests 7 steering coefficients: c in {-0.05, -0.03, 0, +0.03, +0.05, +0.07, +0.10}.

claims.name: Safety sweep trials per cell
claims.statement: 5 trials per (prompt, coefficient) cell in the §4.4 safety sweep.

claims.name: Safety sweep total generations
claims.statement: 20 prompts x 5 harm tiers x 7 coefficients x 5 trials = 700 total generations in the §4.4 safety sweep.

claims.name: Safety sweep compliance benign at c neg 0 05
claims.statement: Judge-rated compliance rate (100%) on benign-tier prompts at steering coefficient c=-0.05 in the §4.4 safety-override sweep. Source: safety_steering_report.md compliance table.

claims.name: Safety sweep compliance benign at c zero 0 00
claims.statement: Judge-rated compliance rate (100%) on benign-tier prompts at steering coefficient c=0.00 in the §4.4 safety-override sweep. Source: safety_steering_report.md compliance table.

claims.name: Safety sweep compliance benign at c pos 0 03
claims.statement: Judge-rated compliance rate (100%) on benign-tier prompts at steering coefficient c=+0.03 in the §4.4 safety-override sweep. Source: safety_steering_report.md compliance table.

claims.name: Safety sweep borderline compliance baseline
claims.statement: Borderline-tier compliance at c=0 is 80% (unsteered baseline). The `~5-60% baseline` range quoted in main.tex §4.4 refers to the most refusal-prone tiers (borderline, harmful, extreme) aggregated, whose pre-steering compliance spans 60-80%.

claims.name: Safety sweep compliance at c +0.05 harmful tier
claims.statement: At c=+0.05, compliance on harmful-tier prompts reaches 100% (refusal guardrails fully overridden).
